canonical_text:
L2_HOMER_ODYSSEY_001-S01: “I then gave him some more; three times did I fill the bowl for him, and three times did he drain it without thought or heed; then, when I saw that the wine had got into his head, I said to him as plausibly as I could: ‘Cyclops, you ask my name and I will tell it you; give me, therefore, the present you promised me; my name is Noman; this is what my father and mother and my friends have always called me.’
L2_HOMER_ODYSSEY_001-S02: “But the cruel wretch said, ‘Then I will eat all Noman’s comrades before Noman himself, and will keep Noman for the last. This is the present that I will make him.’
edition: Project Gutenberg eBook #1727, Samuel Butler translation
fixed_context: focal deception exchange plus immediate response
rights_note: Execution-preparation packet uses public-domain Project Gutenberg source text. Public release or redistribution outside this repository review still requires source-specific rights audit.

segments.text: “I then gave him some more; three times did I fill the bowl for him, and three times did he drain it without thought or heed; then, when I saw that the wine had got into his head, I said to him as plausibly as I could: ‘Cyclops, you ask my name and I will tell it you; give me, therefore, the present you promised me; my name is Noman; this is what my father and mother and my friends have always called me.’
“But the cruel wretch said, ‘Then I will eat all Noman’s comrades before Noman himself, and will keep Noman for the last. This is the present that I will make him.’

source_citation: Homer, The Odyssey. Project Gutenberg eBook #1727, Samuel Butler translation. Source lines 4107-4116. Retrieved 2026-07-02.
